what: Annualised Fixed-Precision Dates and Durations for Actuarial Experience Analysis
title: Annualised Fixed-Precision Dates and Durations for Actuarial Experience Analysis
text: goal datey … standardise annual, .e. year-based calculations e.g. required actuarial mortality experience analyses valuations. Fixed precision dates calendar years [1000, 3000) Technically Proleptic Gregorian calendar Guarantees: Can represent start middle day leap year non leap year Years divisible 120 can convert standard base R dates (Date, POSIXct POSIXlt). Key argument day_fraction. Simpler just use start_day, mid_day end_day. instance start E2R typically included, use start_day. individual survived end E2R also typically included, use end_day. means E2R comprises separate periods interlock correctly (E2Rs measurable) individual died average final day use mid_day.

title: Annualised Fixed-Precision Dates and Durations for Actuarial Experience Analysis

title: Annualised Fixed-Precision Dates and Durations for Actuarial Experience Analysis
text: basic example shows solve common problem: special using README.Rmd instead just README.md? can include R chunks like : ’ll still need render README.Rmd regularly, keep README.md --date. devtools::build_readme() handy . can also embed plots man/figures folder, example:  case, don’t forget commit push resulting figure files, display GitHub CRAN.
code: library(datey) ## basic example code summary(cars) #>      speed           dist        #>  Min.   : 4.0   Min.   :  2.00   #>  1st Qu.:12.0   1st Qu.: 26.00   #>  Median :15.0   Median : 36.00   #>  Mean   :15.4   Mean   : 42.98   #>  3rd Qu.:19.0   3rd Qu.: 56.00   #>  Max.   :25.0   Max.   :120.00

what: Convert an object to a datey — as_datey
title: Convert an object to a datey — as_datey
text: S3 generic. package provides methods following classes: double integer – value interpreted specified calendar year, fractional part representing fraction year. instance, as_datey(2000.5) means halfway though year 2000. (integer means start calendar year, e.g. as_datey(2000L) means start year 2000. datey, Date POSIXct POSIXlt interpreted fractional years. day_fraction argument provided day fraction determined hours, minutes, seconds. instance, as_datey(.POSIXct("2000-03-21 12:00")) means middle 2000-03-21. Note standard use, Date fractional part therefore means start day. instance, as_datey(.Date("2000-03-21 12:00")) means start 2000-03-21. character – day_fraction provided text format must ISO 8601 extended format, .e. YYYY-MM-DD. day_fraction provided text format must YYYY-MM-DD.FFF, .FFF day fraction must present even fraction 0, e.g. "2000-01-01.0" start 1 January 2000.

title: Convert an object to a datey — as_datey
code: as_datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, ...)  # Default S3 method as_datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, ...)  # S3 method for class 'datey' as_datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, ...)  # S3 method for class 'integer' as_datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, ...)  # S3 method for class 'double' as_datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, ...)  # S3 method for class 'character' as_datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, ...)  # S3 method for class 'Date' as_datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, ...)  # S3 method for class 'POSIXct' as_datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, ...)  # S3 method for class 'POSIXlt' as_datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, ...)  as_start_day(x, ...)  as_mid_day(x, ...)  as_end_day(x, ...)

title: Convert an object to a datey — as_datey
text: x vector S3 class. day_fraction day_fraction override. Defaults NULL. day_fraction provided x used derive calendar year, month, day day fraction. day_fraction provided x used solely derive calendar year, month day, day_fraction provides position day. day_fraction must lie inclusive interval [0,1], 0 meaning start day, 0.5 meaning middle day, 1 meaning end day (identical start next day). ... arguments (used package).

what: Get year, month day and day fraction for a datey — as_ymdf
title: Get year, month day and day fraction for a datey — as_ymdf
text: as_ymdf() returns list year, month, day day_fraction breakdown datey, year integer [1000,3000), month integer [1,12], day integer [1,N], N number days month specified year month, day_fraction double [0,1) representing fraction day, e.g. 0 means start 0.5 means middle day. datey constructed using end_day day_fraction = 1 as_ymdf() return start next day day_fraction = 0.

title: Get year, month day and day fraction for a datey — as_ymdf

title: Create a datey — datey
text: Create datey year, month, day , optionally, day-fraction. general, prefer explicit start_day(), mid_day() end_day() versions. deconstruct datey, use as_ymdf().

title: Create a datey — datey
code: datey(year, month, day, day_fraction)  start_day(year, month, day)  mid_day(year, month, day)  end_day(year, month, day)

title: Create a datey — datey
text: year Calendar year. Valid years 1000 (inclusive) 3000 (exclusive). Doubles truncated integers. month Month number calendar year, 1 representing January. Doubles truncated integers. day Day number month, 1 representing first day month. Doubles truncated integers. day_fraction fraction day, [0,1]. 0 means start day, 0.5 means middle day, 1 means end day (identical start next day).

what: Format or print a datey — format.datey
title: Format or print a datey — format.datey
text: datey printed either YYYY-MM-DD, .e. ISO 8601 extended date format, YYYY-MM-DD.FFF .FFF day fraction part (included even day fraction 0). Note datey created end day (day fraction 1) print start following day.

title: Format or print a datey — format.datey
code: # S3 method for class 'datey' format(x, include_day_fraction = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'datey' print(x, include_day_fraction = TRUE, max = NULL, ...)

title: Format or print a datey — format.datey
text: x datey print format. include_day_fraction Whether include fractional day part. Defaults FALSE. ... arguments passed methods. max Numeric NULL, specifying maximal number entries printed. NULL, getOption("max.print") used. Defaults NULL.

what: Is x a leap year? — is_leap_year
title: Is x a leap year? — is_leap_year
text: Tests whether date year leap year. years outside [1000,3000), returns NA. S3 generic. package provides methods : numeric types double integer (interpreted years), date types datey, Date, POSIXct POSIXlt.
